channel.title: Comments for page "Dr. Robinson's Statement"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "Dr. Robinson's Statement"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yeah, the tone here is excellent. The way the story is put together is superb, and the whole thing is well-written.

+1

Edit: Seriously, the tone here is so good, I read this before I write essays so the detached, clinical tone rubs off on me.

item.title: 200th Upvote & 40th Reply!
item.content:encoded: Well done, Smapti. It just goes to show that some people are better off not knowing certain things, even especially if its an anomaly involving them…

item.title: welp
item.content:encoded: I've read entries and documents on this site since early 2012 and made a wikidot account in 2014, only just becoming a member of The Foundation site this year without even thinking of searching for Dr. Robinson on here until now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What is Yehom té eha

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I wonder what Reagan thought of SCP-1981-1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Dude, this is superb. This is how you take a real life event and give it a Foundationverse explanation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This ties in with one of the things I like most about this tale: It shows the Foundation as somewhat vulnerable.
As in, the thing keeping the universe from exploding several times over is not an all-powerful entity; it's just a organization like any other, albeit more secret.

An amazing tale by all accounts. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"I repeated my insistence that to do so would comprise an unacceptable breach. O5-7 informed me that tensions between the Foundation and the United States government were high due to political issues beyond my clearance level." This is in late 1993.

I've decided that the tensions between the Foundation and the US government resulted from the events of http://www.scp-wiki.net/transcript-of-telephone-conversation-august-9-1991 (the Foundation helped themselves to a US Navy nuclear attack submarine).
But it did make it eventually. Congratulations. Excellent piece of work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This remains my favorite Tale on the entire site. Well done, Smapti.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There is no canon. I personally think that hyphenating site numbers looks clunky, so I don't do it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hm, I like it. I do have one little quibble though, usually site designations are hyphenated. So Site 73 should probably be Site-73.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Reading your comment, all I can think is "Reagan smash!"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Okay, this is how to do a piece giving an anomalous explanation to real-life events. This is very interesting and very well executed.

spoiler
– hide block

I felt uncomfortable with the idea of giving Reagan's real-life illness an "SCP did it" explanation, but you did it well enough that I liked it anyway.

I just kept thinking to myself the whole time: Man. Poor Reagan.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: http://i.imgur.com/6d1ql.png

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm throwing money at my screen, yet nothing is happening.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I just tried to upvote this a second time. I would upvote this dozens of time if given the chance.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fantastic and horrifying. I saw you decided to tie it in with the Hanged King after all (and "Yehom té ehal"? Isn't that from the prehistoric things circling the equator?) The historical context is just awesome, and I agree, I like seeing things about the people involved with the Foundation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Make it so.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's not necessarily about the SPC

Sequel idea: Reagan punching sharks.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is exactly what the Ethics Committee deals with. Sometimes they show up in your office and ask you to explain what happened.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Also, the tone is perfect. This is an affidavit prepared in anticipation of litigation. They all read almost precisely like this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: These are the types of stories I really enjoy reading. It's not necessarily about the SPC itself, but about the researches and people whose lives it affects.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I had pictured it as being sort of the opposite, with potency increasing in orders of magnitude with each class. Class A being just like you said, maybe with hypnotic suggestion so that the subject represses or at least develops a strong aversion to thinking about the missing gaps in their memory. Class B inducing a hollywood style mindwipe where the subject has a complete loss of episodic memory, and Class C just being a low intensity stroke, with the subject losing motor and language function and likely having difficulty in storing or creating new memories.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's just how memory loss works in general though, isn't it? As paraphrased from Eternal Sunshine of the Spotless Mind, a little bit of Class A shouldn't be any more harmful then a night of heavy drinking.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Upvoted with great prejudice.

It isn't even so much the subject matter as it is the tone. The tone is excellent.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
In my head, and what seems to be more-or-less the somewhat more common things, goes as follows:

Class A causes a "hard reset." Anything up to a certain amount of time is wiped more-or-less clean, with the dosage amount figuring in the amount of time wiped out.

Class B partially wipes and leaves the recipient confused and impressionable; this is more like the Neurolizer from the MIB movies/cartoon, the people might vaguely recall something going on, but the cover story overrides it (Dude, I saw this car drive straight through a wall today! Well.. I mean yeah the wall was messed up, but I swear that car looked like it didn't have a scratch on it, still had to get towed away though so I guess it just had a hard chassis).

Class C are the weakest of the bunch, mostly affecting short-term memory loss and the like. These are good when that poor bastard with the mouths in his palms gets out and someone spots him eating a burger at a diner without putting it in his face. They just straight don't remember it because it was some "corner of the eye" thing, not enough to make a huge impression, and they basically figured their eyes played tricks on them.

Of course, all of these affect the brain, and there's always the chance of unexpected side-effects since everyone reacts to medication differently. And overdose is ''never'' a good thing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: In my headcanon, amnesiacs cause brain damage, they are known to cause brain damage by Foundation medical personnel, and this information is restricted to medical personnel and Class 4 staff. Even at that cost, it is sometimes better that we forget.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why this is superb! It isn't exactly my headcanon but it's well written enough to stand on it's own. It does make me wish there was some sort of clear delineation in the differences between Class-A, B and C amnesiacs.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't know, I don't think you matched the tone of the transcripts in the actual article. Just doesn't engage for some reason.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That makes the first two even better!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Heh.
This is how to do future prediction in an SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Duuude! That's harsh! I was thinking it was going to be Casey Anthony or someone like that, but poor Taylor Swift? Maybe that's why Kanye got so animated. HE KNEW, people. HE KNEW.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: For the record, the stockbroker's daughter is intended to be Taylor Swift. Who hasn't done anything particularly world-shattering yet, but who knows what events post-2012 video-Reagan is aware of?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So the civil rights lawyer is Barack Obama and the 15 year old Norwegian is Anders Behring Breivik, but who's the stockbroker's daughter? Excellent tale, the effect upon President Reagan the video had adds even more to the already considerable creepy factor of the original SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it. Nicely written and a good accompaniment to SCP-1981. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nicely written. Takes a horrible event and makes it seem almost…commonplace. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like tales and I'd like to see more like it. The Foundation interacting with the government just seems so fun to me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It dragged on a little in the beginning and again near the middle. I kind of wish there was more focus on Reagan and his viewings rather than the experiences of Dr. Robinson in regards to those events.

Still it is well written and at the very least I think it deserves my upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it, it's very good, although some might balk at the idea of writing Reagan's Alzheimers into a Foundation Tale. Bloody good idea, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is my first attempt at writing a Tale. It is based on the immensely popular SCP-1981, and it is composed with the author's approval.

I don't expect it to hit +100 in under 24 hours, but I think it makes a nice compliment to the original.
